meta.name: 5k Performance
meta.subtitle: Break 20 minutes over 16 weeks
meta.description: A serious 16-week performance plan for runners who already have a solid aerobic base and are chasing a sub-20 minute 5k. Five running days per week, two gym sessions, and a structured combination of VO2max intervals, lactate threshold runs and aerobic base work.
meta.coach_notes: Sub-20 requires running 4:00/km for 5km. That pace will feel uncomfortably fast early in the plan — it is supposed to. The plan builds the physiological capacity gradually so that by race week, 4:00/km feels like a pace you can sustain. Do not skip the easy days and do not turn them into moderate days.

weeks.title: Base establishment
weeks.note: First week is about establishing your baseline and getting the body used to five running days. Nothing hard — the difficult sessions start week 2. Use this week to check your easy pace is genuinely easy (fully conversational, feel like you could go for hours).

sessions.det: 6km easy · 5:45–6:10/km · Shakeout pace · Conversational throughout — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

nut.d: 300ml water. Easy shakeout to start the week.
20g protein. Light stretching — calves, hamstrings, IT band.

sessions.det: 2km WU · 8x400m @ current 5k pace (90sec rec) · 2km CD · 10km total · Controlled — assess your current fitness honestly

nut.d: 400ml water. Light banana 30min before if needed — interval sessions need fuel.
25g protein within 30 minutes. Note your split times — they become your progress reference.
500ml water. Rest day — important on a 5-day plan.

sessions.det: 2km WU · 3x8min @ threshold (4:20–4:30/km, 2min jog rec) · 2km CD · 10km total · Sustained effort — should feel like 7/10

nut.d: 400ml water. Threshold runs benefit from being well-fuelled — light breakfast 90 minutes before.
25g protein. Threshold sessions are highly productive — prioritise your recovery afterwards.

sessions.det: Goblet squat 2x12 · Romanian deadlift 2x12 · Reverse lunge 2x12 · Calf raise 3x15 · Core circuit 2x30s — Your first lower body strength session. Keep loads light — this week is about learning the movements. Lower body strength is the foundation of running economy.

8km easy · 6x100m strides at 5k pace within km 6–7 · 5:45–6:00/km easy sections — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

nut.d: Eat 60–90 minutes before. Good carbohydrate meal — long run tomorrow.
Light protein. Hydrate well for tomorrow.

sessions.det: 13km easy · 5:50–6:15/km · Conversational · Last 2km can drift to moderate if feeling strong — A shorter long run this week — still builds time on feet and aerobic base without adding to accumulated fatigue.

nut.d: Porridge with banana and honey. 600ml water. 90 minutes before.
Full recovery meal — protein, carbohydrates, vegetables. This is your most important weekly recovery window.

weeks.title: VO2max introduction
weeks.note: Your first proper VO2max session on Tuesday. The 1km reps should be run at a pace you could just about sustain for 8–10 minutes in a race — approximately 3:45–3:55/km for most athletes targeting sub-20. This pace will feel very uncomfortable. That is correct.

sessions.det: 7km easy · 5:50–6:15/km · Conversational · True recovery pace — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.
2km WU · 5x1km @ 3:48–3:55/km (3min jog rec) · 2km CD · 11km total · Hard but controlled — you should finish rep 5 at the same pace as rep 1

nut.d: 400ml water. Banana or toast 60–90 minutes before — VO2max sessions demand proper fuel.
30g protein immediately. VO2max sessions create significant muscle stress — this is your highest priority recovery window of the week.
500ml water. Full rest. Your body is processing yesterday's work.

sessions.det: 2km WU · 3x10min @ threshold (4:15–4:25/km, 90sec jog rec) · 2km CD · 11km total — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.

Back squat 3x12 · Romanian deadlift 3x12 · Bulgarian split squat 3x12 · Calf raise 3x15 · Core circuit 3x12s — Lower body strength is the engine of your running economy. These compound lifts build the hip and quad strength that keeps your form together in the final miles.

9km easy · 6x100m strides built into the run — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

14km easy · 5:50–6:15/km · Aerobic base run · Fully conversational — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.

weeks.title: Volume build
weeks.note: Volume reaches its base-phase peak this week. The Wednesday rest day is deliberate — your legs need it between two hard days. If the VO2max session on Tuesday leaves you unable to run Thursday's threshold session well, the Tuesday pace was too fast.

sessions.det: 8km easy · 5:50–6:15/km — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.
2km WU · 6x1km @ 3:48–3:55/km (3min jog rec) · 2km CD · 12km total · One more rep than last week — same pace

nut.d: 30g protein immediately. Six 1km reps at VO2max pace is serious work — take the recovery seriously.
500ml water. Rest. Full recovery before Thursday.

sessions.det: 2km WU · 2x15min @ threshold (4:12–4:22/km, 2min jog rec) · 2km CD · 12km total — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.

nut.d: 400ml water. 15-minute blocks are proper threshold training — these build your lactate threshold directly.

sessions.det: Pull-ups 3x12 · DB row 3x12 · Overhead press 3x12 · Face pulls 3x12 · Core circuit 3x12s — Strong upper body and core keeps your posture tall when you tire. These sessions directly improve arm drive efficiency and reduce the slumping that costs you energy late in a race.
9km easy · 8x100m strides — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

nut.d: 60–90 minutes before. Good carbohydrates for tomorrow.

sessions.det: 15km easy · 5:50–6:15/km · Final 3km can push to marathon pace if feeling strong — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 600ml water. 90 minutes before.
Full recovery meal. Deload next week — you have earned it.

weeks.title: Deload week 1
weeks.note: Volume drops to 55% of last week. Keep the effort easy throughout. The VO2max and threshold adaptations from weeks 1–3 consolidate now. Your legs may feel unusually good mid-week — that is the adaptation working.

sessions.det: 5km easy · Very relaxed — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

nut.d: 300ml water. Deload week — everything is lighter.

sessions.det: 1.5km WU · 6x200m at 5k pace (90sec rec) · 1.5km CD · 6km total · Just keeping the neuromuscular system sharp — Intervals build your VO2max ceiling and teach your body to run efficiently at race pace. Recovery between reps should be easy enough that you can complete every interval at the target pace.

2km WU · 10min moderate tempo · 2km CD · 6km total · Just maintaining the feel — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.

Hip thrust 2x12 · Nordic curl 2x12 · Lateral band walk 2x12 · Calf raise 3x15 · Stretching circuit 10min — A gentle posterior chain session and mobility work. During taper this is about keeping the body feeling loose and activated, not adding load.
5km easy — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.
9km easy · 6:00–6:15/km · Relaxed — A shorter long run this week — still builds time on feet and aerobic base without adding to accumulated fatigue.

nut.d: Normal recovery meal. Phase 2 begins next week.

weeks.title: Intensity increases
weeks.note: Back to full intensity with a harder edge. The 1200m reps are the key session — they develop the specific endurance needed to hold 4:00/km for 5km. Pace judgement is critical: rep 1 at the same pace as rep 5.

sessions.det: 8km easy · 5:50–6:15/km — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

2km WU · 5x1200m @ 3:50–3:58/km (3.5min jog rec) · 2km CD · 13km total · Longer reps develop specific 5k endurance — Intervals build your VO2max ceiling and teach your body to run efficiently at race pace. Recovery between reps should be easy enough that you can complete every interval at the target pace.

nut.d: 400ml water. Substantial breakfast 90 minutes before — 1200m reps are demanding.
30g protein immediately. 5x1200 is close to 6km of quality running — respect the recovery.

sessions.det: 2km WU · 25min continuous @ threshold (4:12–4:22/km) · 2km CD · ~11km total — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.

nut.d: 25g protein. Continuous threshold is harder mentally than intervals — well done for staying in it.

sessions.det: Pull-ups 3x12 · DB row 3x12 · Overhead press 3x12 · Face pulls 3x12 · Core circuit 3x12s — Strong upper body and core keeps your posture tall when you tire. These sessions directly improve arm drive efficiency and reduce the slumping that costs you energy late in a race.
9km easy · 8x100m strides at 5k pace — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

15km · First 11km easy (5:55–6:15/km) · Final 4km at marathon pace (~4:30/km) · Progression run — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 90 minutes before. Consider a gel at 10km.
Full recovery meal. Progression runs are highly productive — fuel them properly.

weeks.title: Race simulation
weeks.note: The Tuesday session this week simulates racing — a continuous 4km at close to 5k race pace. This is the closest thing to a practice race in the plan. Your goal is to run 4km at 4:05/km and feel like you could continue for one more kilometre. If it feels impossible, the pace needs adjustment.

sessions.det: 8km easy · 5:50–6:15/km — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

2km WU · 4km @ goal 5k pace (4:00–4:05/km) as a continuous effort · 2km CD · 8km total · This is your current fitness test — Intervals build your VO2max ceiling and teach your body to run efficiently at race pace. Recovery between reps should be easy enough that you can complete every interval at the target pace.

nut.d: 400ml water. Treat this like a race — proper pre-session nutrition and warm-up.
30g protein. Your 4km time tells you exactly where you are. If you hit 4:00/km, the sub-20 is on track.

sessions.det: 2km WU · 2x18min @ threshold (4:10–4:20/km, 2min rec) · 2km CD · 12km total — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.

Back squat 4x8 · Romanian deadlift 4x8 · Bulgarian split squat 4x8 · Calf raise 3x15 · Core circuit 4x8s — Lower body strength is the engine of your running economy. These compound lifts build the hip and quad strength that keeps your form together in the final miles.
9km easy · 8x100m strides — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.
16km easy · 5:55–6:20/km · Your longest run of the plan — keep it genuinely easy

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 600ml water. 90 minutes before. Gel at 10km.
Full recovery meal. Deload next week.

weeks.title: Deload week 2
weeks.note: Second deload. Volume back to 55%. You are now at the halfway point — the base phase is complete. The speed and race-specific phase begins week 8. Rest well.

sessions.det: 5km easy · Very relaxed — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

1.5km WU · 6x300m at 5k pace (90sec rec) · 1.5km CD · 6km total — Intervals build your VO2max ceiling and teach your body to run efficiently at race pace. Recovery between reps should be easy enough that you can complete every interval at the target pace.

2km WU · 12min easy tempo · 2km CD · 6km total — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.

Hip thrust 2x12 · Nordic curl 2x12 · Lateral band walk 2x12 · Calf raise 3x15 · Stretching circuit 10min — A gentle posterior chain session and mobility work. During taper this is about keeping the body feeling loose and activated, not adding load.
5km easy — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.
9km easy · Relaxed and comfortable — A shorter long run this week — still builds time on feet and aerobic base without adding to accumulated fatigue.

nut.d: Normal recovery meal. Phase 2 — speed and race specificity — begins next week.

weeks.title: Speed phase — shorter faster
weeks.note: Phase 2 introduces shorter, faster intervals. The 300m reps on Tuesday are run at mile pace — significantly faster than 5k pace. This trains your legs to turn over at a higher cadence, which makes 4:00/km feel more natural by comparison.

sessions.det: 8km easy · 5:50–6:15/km — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.
2km WU · 10x300m @ 3:25–3:35/km (2min walk rec) · 2km CD · 10km total · Fast and powerful — max 10 reps total

nut.d: 400ml water. Speed sessions are neurologically demanding — arrive fresh.
25g protein. Fast twitch work takes longer to recover from than endurance intervals.

sessions.det: 2km WU · 30min @ threshold (4:10–4:20/km) · 2km CD · 12km total · Building toward race pace endurance — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.

Back squat 3x12 · Romanian deadlift 3x12 · Bulgarian split squat 3x12 · Calf raise 3x15 · Core circuit 3x12s — Lower body strength is the engine of your running economy. These compound lifts build the hip and quad strength that keeps your form together in the final miles.
9km easy · 8x100m strides — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

15km · First 11km easy · Final 4km at 4:05/km (race pace) · Progression run — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.

weeks.title: Mixed speed and threshold
weeks.note: The combination session on Tuesday — mixing 400m reps and 200m reps in the same workout — trains your ability to change pace. This is a key 5k skill. Run the 400s at 5k pace and the 200s noticeably faster.

sessions.det: 8km easy · 5:50–6:15/km — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

2km WU · 4x(400m @ 5k pace, 90sec rec, 200m @ mile pace, 2min rec) · 2km CD · 10km total — Intervals build your VO2max ceiling and teach your body to run efficiently at race pace. Recovery between reps should be easy enough that you can complete every interval at the target pace.

nut.d: 400ml water. Combination sessions are technically demanding — focus on the pace transitions.
25g protein. Changing pace is a learnable skill and you are getting better at it.

sessions.det: 2km WU · 2x18min @ threshold (4:08–4:18/km, 90sec rec) · 2km CD · 12km total — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.

Pull-ups 3x12 · DB row 3x12 · Overhead press 3x12 · Face pulls 3x12 · Core circuit 3x12s — Strong upper body and core keeps your posture tall when you tire. These sessions directly improve arm drive efficiency and reduce the slumping that costs you energy late in a race.
9km easy · 8x100m strides — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

15km easy · 5:55–6:15/km · Aerobic base run — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.

weeks.title: Deload week 3
weeks.note: Third deload. Volume drops. You are three quarters of the way through the plan — the race is six weeks away. Rest well, eat well, sleep well. The hard work is already in the bank.

sessions.det: 5km easy — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

2km easy · 8x100m strides at 5k pace (full walk rec) · 2km easy · 5km total — Intervals build your VO2max ceiling and teach your body to run efficiently at race pace. Recovery between reps should be easy enough that you can complete every interval at the target pace.

2km WU · 12min easy tempo · 2km CD · 6km total — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.

Hip thrust 2x12 · Nordic curl 2x12 · Lateral band walk 2x12 · Calf raise 3x15 · Stretching circuit 10min — A gentle posterior chain session and mobility work. During taper this is about keeping the body feeling loose and activated, not adding load.
5km easy — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.
9km easy · Relaxed — A shorter long run this week — still builds time on feet and aerobic base without adding to accumulated fatigue.

nut.d: Normal recovery meal. Peak block next — six weeks to race day.

weeks.title: Peak block — week 1
weeks.note: The peak block. Four weeks of maximum quality before the taper. The 1600m reps on Tuesday are your most race-specific session of the plan — each rep is roughly one lap of a track at race pace. This develops the ability to sustain 4:00/km under fatigue.

sessions.det: 8km easy · 5:50–6:15/km — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

2km WU · 4x1600m @ 3:55–4:02/km (4min jog rec) · 2km CD · 13km total · This is the cornerstone session of the plan — Peak-phase intervals — this is where your fitness is tested. These are hard but controlled. If the pace slips, the session is done.

nut.d: 400ml water. Substantial breakfast 90 minutes before — 4x1600 is close to 10km of quality running.
30g protein immediately. This session is the hardest of the plan — give it proper recovery.
500ml water. Full rest. Your body needs it.

sessions.det: 2km WU · 2x20min @ threshold (4:08–4:18/km, 2min rec) · 2km CD · 13km total — Threshold work at peak phase. Your lactate threshold determines the pace you can sustain for an hour. These sessions push that ceiling up.

nut.d: 25g protein. 2x20min threshold is elite-level training. You are ready for it.

sessions.det: Pull-ups 4x5 · DB row 4x5 · Overhead press 4x5 · Face pulls 4x5 · Core circuit 4x5s — Upper body work while your legs taper — good timing. Pull movements build the postural strength that holds your form together at race pace.
9km easy · 8x100m strides — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

15km · First 9km easy · 4x2km at 4:05/km with 1km easy between · 2km easy cool-down — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. Gel at 8km and 12km.
Full recovery meal. Three more quality weeks.

weeks.title: Peak block — week 2
weeks.note: Second peak week. Same structure as last week — trust the repetition. The adaptations from repeating the same sessions are cumulative. Note your splits from week 11 and aim to match or better them.

sessions.det: 8km easy · 5:50–6:15/km — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

2km WU · 5x1200m @ 3:52–4:00/km (3.5min jog rec) · 2km CD · 13km total — Peak-phase intervals — this is where your fitness is tested. These are hard but controlled. If the pace slips, the session is done.

2km WU · 35min @ threshold (4:08–4:18/km) · 2km CD · 13km total · Your longest threshold run — Threshold work at peak phase. Your lactate threshold determines the pace you can sustain for an hour. These sessions push that ceiling up.

nut.d: 25g protein. 35 minutes of continuous threshold is a significant achievement.

sessions.det: Pull-ups 4x5 · DB row 4x5 · Overhead press 4x5 · Face pulls 4x5 · Core circuit 4x5s — Upper body work while your legs taper — good timing. Pull movements build the postural strength that holds your form together at race pace.
9km easy · 8x100m strides — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.
14km easy · 5:55–6:20/km · Aerobic base run — save the race pace for the track sessions

weeks.title: Peak block — week 3
weeks.note: Third and final peak week. Volume is slightly reduced to allow the fitness to peak. The interval session shifts back to 1km reps at race pace — controlled, confident and feeling strong. Three weeks until race day.

sessions.det: 7km easy · 5:50–6:15/km — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

2km WU · 6x1km @ exact 5k race pace (3min rec) · 2km CD · 12km total · Confident, controlled, owning the pace — Sharp intervals close to race week keep your legs feeling quick without adding volume. The fitness is already built — this is a reminder, not a workout.

nut.d: 400ml water. Race pace should feel familiar now — this is your pace.
25g protein. You are ready for this race. The fitness is there.

sessions.det: 2km WU · 25min @ threshold · 2km CD · 10km total — A short threshold session to maintain the feel of race effort. Comfortably hard — not racing.

Pull-ups 4x5 · DB row 4x5 · Overhead press 4x5 · Face pulls 4x5 · Core circuit 4x5s — Upper body work while your legs taper — good timing. Pull movements build the postural strength that holds your form together at race pace.
8km easy · 6x100m strides — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.
13km easy · 5:55–6:20/km · Comfortable and controlled — A confidence long run in taper — resist the urge to push. Arrive at the start line rested, not tired from a final fitness test.

nut.d: Full recovery meal. Two more weeks.

weeks.title: Taper — week 1
weeks.note: Taper begins. Volume drops by 35%. Intensity stays. The goal this week is to maintain sharpness while letting the accumulated fatigue drain. Your legs will feel sluggish for the first few days of a taper — this is normal and temporary.

sessions.det: 6km easy · 5:50–6:15/km — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

nut.d: 300ml water. Taper begins — the work is done.

sessions.det: 1.5km WU · 6x400m @ race pace (2min rec) · 1.5km CD · 7km total · Sharp and confident — Sharp intervals close to race week keep your legs feeling quick without adding volume. The fitness is already built — this is a reminder, not a workout.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest. Your legs may feel heavy — the taper is working.

sessions.det: 2km WU · 15min @ tempo · 2km CD · 7km total — A short threshold session to maintain the feel of race effort. Comfortably hard — not racing.

Hip thrust 2x12 · Nordic curl 2x12 · Lateral band walk 2x12 · Calf raise 3x15 · Stretching circuit 10min — A gentle posterior chain session and mobility work. During taper this is about keeping the body feeling loose and activated, not adding load.
6km easy · 6x100m strides — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.
10km easy · 6:00–6:20/km · Comfortable — A shorter long run this week — still builds time on feet and aerobic base without adding to accumulated fatigue.

nut.d: Normal recovery meal. One more week.

weeks.title: Taper — week 2
weeks.note: Second taper week. Volume drops to 40% of peak. By mid-week your legs should feel excellent — light, fast and ready. If you feel like you are losing fitness, you are not. The freshness you feel is the adaptation completing.

sessions.det: 5km easy · Comfortable — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

1.5km WU · 4x400m @ race pace (2min rec) · 1.5km CD · 5km total · Fast, light, sharp — Sharp intervals close to race week keep your legs feeling quick without adding volume. The fitness is already built — this is a reminder, not a workout.

nut.d: 300ml water. Four short reps — arrive fresh, leave fresh.
Light protein. You should feel good. That is the point.

sessions.det: 4km easy · Very comfortable · Your legs should feel great — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

Hip thrust 2x12 · Nordic curl 2x12 · Lateral band walk 2x12 · Calf raise 3x15 · Stretching circuit 10min — A gentle posterior chain session and mobility work. During taper this is about keeping the body feeling loose and activated, not adding load.
4km easy — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

nut.d: Light protein. Race week starts tomorrow.

sessions.det: 5km easy · Comfortable · Arrive at race week fresh — A shorter long run this week — still builds time on feet and aerobic base without adding to accumulated fatigue.

nut.d: Normal meal. One week to race day. You are ready.

weeks.title: Race week — sub-20 attempt
weeks.note: Race week. Everything this week is in service of Saturday. Sleep well, eat well, keep the legs moving but nothing hard. You have done 15 weeks of serious training. The sub-20 is inside you — Saturday is when you release it.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest. Race week — stay off your feet as much as possible.
Increase carbohydrate intake slightly this week. Pasta, rice, bread with each meal. Avoid anything new or heavy.

sessions.det: 10min easy · 4x200m @ race pace (full walk rec) · 5min easy · 4km total · Wake the legs up — nothing more

nut.d: Light protein snack. Good carbohydrate meal today.
500ml water. Rest. Stay off your feet.
High carbohydrate meals. Pasta or rice at lunch and dinner. Nothing that might cause GI issues.

sessions.det: 3km easy · 3x100m strides · Done — legs should feel electric

nut.d: Light protein. Race dinner tonight — high carbohydrate, early, familiar.
Pasta or rice. Eat by 19:00. No alcohol. Nothing new. This is race prep now.
500ml water. Complete rest. Lay your kit out. Know your route to the start line.
Normal meals. Keep carbohydrates high. Drink consistently — 2.5 litres today. Avoid alcohol, caffeine after 14:00, anything unfamiliar.
Lay out kit: vest, shorts, shoes, watch, safety pins, race number. Set alarm 2.5 hours before race start. You have done everything right — now trust it.

sessions.det: 2km easy warm-up · 4x100m strides · Race: go through 1km at 4:00, hold 4:00 through 3km, build to 3:55 final 2km if you have it · Negative split target — Race day. Trust your training. Start conservatively — most races are won or lost in the second half.

nut.d: Porridge with banana and honey, or two slices of toast with peanut butter. 500ml water. Something you have eaten before a race before — no experiments today.
300ml water. Sports drink optional if you normally use one. Nothing new.
Start your 2km warm-up. The warm-up matters enormously for a 5k — your body needs to be ready to run hard from km 1. Include drills and strides.
100ml water. Get to the start line early. Run the race in your head: 4:00 through 1km, hold it, build from 3km.
Go through 1km at exactly 4:00. It will feel controlled. That is correct. The runners who go out at 3:50 will come back to you.
At 3km, you have 2km left. If you are on pace and feeling strong, begin the build. If struggling, hold your pace. Never slow down by more than 5sec/km.
Water at the finish line — 400ml in the first 10 minutes. Keep moving for 10 minutes to cool down.
Banana and protein bar. Start replenishing quickly.
You just ran sub-20. Eat whatever you want — protein, carbohydrates and a well-earned treat. Drink consistently through the afternoon. You have arrived.
500ml water. Gentle walk. You ran sub-20 minutes for 5km. Whatever comes next, you have proven you can do it.
